stimulus: The field of paleoclimatology owes much to British ______blank Nicholas Shackleton, a pioneering isotope geochemist whose careful analyses of seafloor sediment cores helped establish the orbital theory of ice ages and reshaped scientific understanding of long-term climate cycles.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. "British geophysicist" is an indefinite role, so the proper name "Nicholas Shackleton" supplies essential identification and is not set off by punctuation.

Choice B is incorrect because a comma wrongly implies that the name could be removed without loss of meaning. Choices C and D are incorrect because a semicolon or colon between the role and the name does not match any standard punctuation pattern between a role and a restrictive proper name.
